title: Dalenia glabra
intro: Dalbergia glabra, also known as Indian Blackwood, is a tree species native to the tropical regions of Asia. This plant is valued for its health benefits, which include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as fever, pain, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Dalbergia glabra include flavonoids, alkaloids, and tannins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Dalbergia glabra are its bark and wood extracts, which are used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, and its essential oil, which is used in aromatherapy.
